---
title: "4a: NMF Decomposition — Phylon Identification"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Summary

Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) decomposes the accessory genome presence/absence matrix into phylon-level structure. Multiple Correspondence Analysis (MCA) guides rank selection, and consensus clustering across gene-frequency submatrices validates the robustness of the identified phylons.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
summary = pd.read_csv("../output/data/4a_nmf_summary.csv")
summary
```

## MCA Cumulative Variance

![](../output/figures/4a_mca_variance.png){width=95%}

## Consensus Matrix (All Submatrices)

![](../output/figures/4a_consensus_clustermap.png){width=80%}

## Consensus Matrix (Filtered)

Excludes high-frequency submatrices (50–100% and 75–100%) which overlap heavily with core genes.

![](../output/figures/4a_consensus_clustermap_filtered.png){width=80%}

## NMF Output Shapes

```{python}
import pandas as pd
L = pd.read_csv("../data/processed/nmf-outputs/L.csv", index_col=0)
A = pd.read_csv("../data/processed/nmf-outputs/A.csv", index_col=0)
print(f"L (genomes × phylons): {L.shape}")
print(f"A (phylons × genes): {A.shape}")
print(f"Phylons identified: {L.shape[1]}")
```
